howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Phayeng
topPlaces.description: Phayeng or Chakpa Phayeng is a large village located in the Lamshang division of Imphal West district, Manipur, India. It is located near the foothill of the Kangchup hill ranges.

topPlaces.name: Kangchup
topPlaces.description: Kangchup is a village in the Lamshang subdivision of Imphal West district, in the Indian state of Manipur. The village and nearby hamlets were impacted by the Meitei–Kuki ethnic violence that started in Manipur in May 2023.

topPlaces.name: Patsoi
topPlaces.description: Patsoi is a tehsil (sub-district) in the Imphal West District of Manipur. The census code of Patsoi block is 01880.

topPlaces.name: Loitang Khunou
topPlaces.description: Loitang Khunou is a village in the Lamshang subdivision of Imphal West district of Manipur, India. It is governed by the Phumlou gram panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Lamshang
topPlaces.description: Lamsang (also spelt as Lamshang) is a town and a Municipal Council in Imphal West district in the Indian state of Manipur.

topPlaces.name: Manipur Zoological Garden
topPlaces.description: Manipur Zoological Garden (Meitei: Manipur Sha-Uchek Thampham) is a zoo in Iroisemba, Manipur. It is the second habitat of Sangai (Cervus eldi eldi), the world's only dancing deer species, after the Keibul Lamjao National Park, the world's only floating national park.

topPlaces.name: Kiyam
topPlaces.description: Kiyam is a village in the Lamshang subdivision of Imphal West, Manipur, India. It is administered by a local Sarpanch.

topPlaces.name: Lamphelpat
topPlaces.description: Lamphelpat (Meitei pronunciation: /ləm.pʰel.pát/) is the district headquarter of Imphal West district in the state of Manipur, India. It is a suburb of Imphal city, the capital of Manipur.

items.note: Phayeng or Chakpa Phayeng is a large village located in the Lamshang division of Imphal West district, Manipur, India. It is located near the foothill of the Kangchup hill ranges.

Kangchup is a village in the Lamshang subdivision of Imphal West district, in the Indian state of Manipur. The village and nearby hamlets were impacted by the Meitei–Kuki ethnic violence that started in Manipur in May 2023.
Patsoi is a tehsil (sub-district) in the Imphal West District of Manipur. The census code of Patsoi block is 01880.
Loitang Khunou is a village in the Lamshang subdivision of Imphal West district of Manipur, India. It is governed by the Phumlou gram panchayat.
Lamsang (also spelt as Lamshang) is a town and a Municipal Council in Imphal West district in the Indian state of Manipur.

Manipur Zoological Garden (Meitei: Manipur Sha-Uchek Thampham) is a zoo in Iroisemba, Manipur. It is the second habitat of Sangai (Cervus eldi eldi), the world's only dancing deer species, after the Keibul Lamjao National Park, the world's only floating national park.
Kiyam is a village in the Lamshang subdivision of Imphal West, Manipur, India. It is administered by a local Sarpanch.
Lamphelpat (Meitei pronunciation: /ləm.pʰel.pát/) is the district headquarter of Imphal West district in the state of Manipur, India. It is a suburb of Imphal city, the capital of Manipur.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bihu Loukon?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bihu Loukon is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bihu Loukon?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Imphal Airport (9 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (123 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bihu Loukon?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bihu Loukon at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bihu Loukon?
faq.a: Stays in Bihu Loukon start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bihu Loukon?
faq.a: The top attractions are Phayeng, Kangchup, Patsoi, Loitang Khunou, Lamshang.
